ArtistIdentity:
I am an artist because silence never worked for me.
Some things don’t fit into normal conversations—so I turn them into melodies.

I’m not here just to make songs, I’m here to translate feelings people struggle to say out loud.
Every line I write, every chord I play—it’s my way of making sense of everything I feel.
This isn’t a phase or a hobby. It’s how I exist.
TrainingDetails:
My journey into music hasn’t been traditional—I didn’t go through formal vocal training or structured guitar classes. Everything I know about singing and playing guitar, I’ve learned on my own through constant practice, trial and error, and a lot of patience.

Music production started the same way. In the beginning, I locked myself in my room, experimenting, failing, and figuring things out piece by piece. Later, I took online classes with Mix With Vasudev, which helped me refine my skills and understand the technical side more deeply. It’s been around two years of consistently working on production.

So far, I’ve released 6 original songs, with 3–4 more tracks completed and several still in progress, lined up for release. Every part of the process—composing, writing, producing, mixing, and mastering—I handle myself.

But if there’s one thing that defines me the most, it’s songwriting. That’s the part that comes most naturally to me. It’s where my thoughts, emotions, and experiences find their place without force. I don’t just write songs—I translate what I feel into something people can hear and connect with.

My journey has been independent, unpolished at times, but real—and that’s exactly what shapes my sound today.
CoreSkills:
My sound is simple, raw, and emotion-driven. I focus on honest melodies and minimal production so the feeling stays real.
My primary weapon is songwriting—it comes naturally to me and reflects my thoughts and emotions in the most authentic way. Everything else—vocals and production—supports the story, not the other way around.
StrengthsUniqueness:
Honestly, nothing I do is completely new—and that’s the truth most artists avoid. My edge isn’t in trying to be different, but in being real without forcing it.
My X-factor is how naturally I turn thoughts and emotions into simple, relatable songs. I don’t overcomplicate or overproduce—I keep it raw so people can truly feel it and connect with it.
DailyTime: On most days, I dedicate around 6–8 hours to my craft.  I start my mornings (7–10 AM) with vocal practice, learning and training through online resources. Then from 11 AM to 12 PM, I focus on guitar practice, again self-taught and consistent.  From the afternoon into the evening (around 1–4/5 PM), I shift into creating—writing songs, producing music, and working on content like reels and promotion for my originals.  It’s a self-built routine, but it keeps me improving every single day.
MentorName: I don’t come from a traditional guru–shishya background. I’ve built most of my skills on my own through consistent practice and learning online.  However, my understanding of music production was shaped further through online mentorship with Mix With Vasudev, which helped refine my approach.
CurrentFans: My audience is mainly 16–25-year-olds who connect with emotional, relatable indie and acoustic music. Most are based in India and discover my work through Instagram and digital platforms.
CurrentSetup:
My setup is simple and self-built. I use FL Studio as my DAW, along with an Audio-Technica AT2020 mic, an M-Audio interface, and Sennheiser headphones.
No awards yet
CurrentlyWorkingOn: Right now, I’m working simultaneously on 1–2 projects, focusing on Indian pop and indie pop vibes. New music is in progress, with upcoming releases in the pipeline.
DailyRituals:
My daily riyaaz starts with 2–3 hours of vocal practice in the morning, followed by about an hour of guitar practice. Later in the day, I spend several hours writing, producing, and working on content.

Beyond music, I also do a 1-hour workout daily, which helps me stay physically active and mentally focused.
LearningNeeds: Right now, I want to improve my understanding of Indian classical music, learn piano, and get better at music marketing so I can grow my audience more effectively.
MentorshipNeeds: I’m looking for practical mentorship to refine my sound and improve my music marketing.
CurationNeeds: Yes, I need help with polishing my sound—especially in production and vocal production. I feel like I’m not consistently achieving the quality I expect, and I want to tighten and refine my overall output.
FandomNeeds: What’s missing right now is a strong, consistent growth system—especially in social media strategy, and the right collaborations. I need a clearer plan to reach more people and convert listeners into a loyal audience.
AspirationalGoal:
One year from now, my goal is to establish myself as one of the biggest Indian indie and pop artists, reaching a massive audience through my music.

I want to perform at major music festivals in India and aim for global stages like Coachella, along with collaborating with artists like Diljit Dosanjh.
AnythingElse:
If I succeed in my music career, I don’t just want success for myself. I want to use it with purpose.
I aim to support environmental causes and contribute towards reducing poverty in whatever ways I can. As long as I’m here, I want my impact to go beyond music.

ArtistIdentity: I am an artist because music became the strongest way for me to express everything I could never say out loud. PNM is the voice of Partik’s real life—the pain, struggles, growth, and experiences that shaped me. I lost my father at the age of 10 and stepped into work from the age of 12, which made me grow up early and sacrifice parts of childhood. A lot of emotions stayed unspoken for years, and music became the place where they could finally live. What started as expression became purpose, and what started as pain became art.
TrainingDetails: My foundation was built through life before it was built through music. Responsibility at a young age gave me discipline, resilience, and maturity. I learned my craft through self-discovery writing lyrics, understanding emotions, sharpening delivery, experimenting with sound, and constantly evolving through experience. Music was never just a hobby for me; it became therapy, identity, and ambition. Over time, I fell in love with the depth of the art itself sometimes complex, sometimes fun, sometimes deeply relatable, and sometimes impossible to explain. That journey continues to shape me as both an artist and a person.
CoreSkills: My primary weapon is lyric writing backed by versatility. I can write layered bars, storytelling records, introspective songs, high-energy rap tracks, and melodic music with equal intent. I focus heavily on rhyme structures, emotion, delivery, and making every line mean something. Whether it’s technical rap or a relatable melody, my strength is turning thoughts and experiences into powerful records.
StrengthsUniqueness: My X-factor is range with authenticity. I can move from hardcore lyrical rap to melody, from pain to confidence, from deep storytelling to fun energy while still sounding like myself. A lot of artists can do one lane well, but I bring multiple dimensions with honesty behind them. What makes me different is that my music comes from lived experience, so no matter the style, it carries truth, emotion, and identity.
DailyTime: every second am thinking about art or making random things rhyme
MentorName: I do not come from a traditional Guru-Shishya background, but life itself has been my greatest teacher.
CurrentFans: My current listeners are mainly in the 18–40 age group who connect with originality, skill, and strong personality in music. A large part of my audience comes from Delhi, New Delhi, Mumbai, and Ludhiana, with listeners from other cities discovering my work through digital platforms as well. My fan base appreciates creative bars, technical lyricism, storytelling, and the confidence-driven energy I bring to records. They enjoy music that balances sharp writing, authenticity, ambition, arrogance, and real-life emotion.
CurrentSetup: My setup is built around a modern independent artist workflow. I record in professional studios using DAWs like FL Studio and other software depending on the project. My main tools are songwriting, vocal performance, and creative direction, along with mobile tools for content and promotion. Achievements so far include winning rap battles, receiving shoutouts from industry celebrities, performing live on notable stages, and building an organic audience independently.
CurrentlyWorkingOn: Right now, I’m creating multiple new singles and collaborating with other creative artists to bring fresh sounds and ideas. Alongside the music, I’m strongly focused on the marketing side of the industry—building strategy, growing visibility, and using social media promotions to expand reach. The current goal is to combine quality music with smart promotion and consistent growth.
DailyRituals: My daily discipline is built around growth, structure, and consistency. I maintain a strict daily schedule, balancing music, health, and mental well-being. Gym is a key part of my routine, helping me stay physically strong, focused, and disciplined. In music, I write regularly, sharpen lyrics, and challenge myself creatively through a “7-minute drill,” where I give myself just seven minutes to create a full song to keep my skills sharp and instincts strong. I also focus on mental health, making sure my mind stays clear, motivated, and ready to perform at a high level.
LearningNeeds: What I’ve learned is that the next step starts with me. I need to keep improving my work ethic, stay consistent in creating new music, and leave the perfectionist mindset behind. For me now, it’s progress over perfection. I’m focused on releasing more, creating momentum, and trusting growth through action. I’m also working on building stronger industry relationships, making connections, and showing up at music events. In my early years, I avoided socializing a lot, but now I understand that visibility, networking, and community are key parts of growth as an artist.
MentorshipNeeds: I’m looking for mentorship from someone who understands the marketing and promotional side of music at a high level. I’m confident in my artistry I know I bring strong lyricism, versatility, and a sound with real potential. What I want guidance on is how to turn that talent into visibility: building the right strategy, reaching the right audience, creating momentum, and converting attention into long term growth. For me, the missing piece is not creativity it’s learning how to get the right eyeballs on the work.
CurationNeeds: My audio quality is already a strong point, and I’m always working to polish it further with every release. Where I see the biggest room for growth is in video content and artwork, where stronger visuals can elevate the overall brand and impact. For live stage design, I’d value more experience and guidance to build a sharper, more memorable performance presence.
FandomNeeds: What’s missing in my growth right now is better distribution and high-level collaborations. Stronger distribution would help the music reach wider and more targeted audiences, while the right collaborations could open new markets, add visibility, and create bigger opportunities. With the talent and content in place, the next level is expanding reach through the right partnerships and systems.
AspirationalGoal: My North Star goal for the next 12 months is to land a breakout record that reaches 10 million streams, while building a catalog that averages 1 million streams across releases. I see myself stepping onto a bigger global stage charting on Billboard, performing major shows, and moving toward headlining iconic venue The O2 Arena. More than numbers, the goal is to create undeniable momentum and establish myself as an artist with worldwide potential.
AnythingElse:
I come from a background where life made me grow up early. Losing my father at a young age and carrying responsibilities from childhood shaped the hunger, discipline, and mindset I carry today. Music was never just entertainment for me it became expression, therapy, and purpose.
I’ve made mistakes, doubted myself, chased perfection too long, and learned lessons the hard way. But every setback built character. Now I understand that talent alone is not enough consistency, growth, relationships, and resilience matter just as much.
My vision is bigger than just releasing songs. I want to inspire people who come from difficult situations to believe they can still create something powerful from pain. ArtistIdentity: I am an artist because that has always been my calling. I cannot imagine myself doing anything other than music. Music is my meditation, my therapy, my way of expressing my inner emotions, desires, and feelings through poetry & melody. Music has always been with me, and I truly believe music has the power to make or break a person. Without music, life would be miserable, and I feel I can contribute by sharing my unique experiences and emotions through music to the world.
TrainingDetails:
These are my formal trainings:
1. Visharad in Tabla (Hindustani Classical Music) for 8 years
2. Trinity Certified Drummer - 4 years
3. Diploma in Audio Engineering and Music Production - 1.5 years
4. B.A degree in performing arts (Specializing in Indian Classical music) from Bhartiya Vidyapeeth, Pune, India
5. Postgraduate degree - MA Sound (Composition, Production & Sound Design) from Bath School of Performing Arts, UK

Other than this, I am a self-taught average pianist and am decent at playing all sorts of percussion instruments, ranging from djembe, cajon, to dholak, pakhawaj, etc. 

I also write Urdu poetry. I've written the lyrics for both my originals in Hindi as well as in English. I've been producing my own music for 3 years now. I have released 2 originals till date and am planning to finish multiple projects this year.
CoreSkills:
Core skills include producing music with a unique sonic palette. 
Also being able to do all the stages of production myself, from composition, recording, lyrics writing, to mixing & mastering & post-production.

I also excel in making fusion music, aiming to blend traditional South-Asian heritage, culture and musical practices with modern production techniques.

I am also able to do good percussion arrangements, build a good song structure, and write the right lyrics to complement the melody.
StrengthsUniqueness:
I consider my ability to understand the structure of a song as my strength. Where to build the energy, where to have an instrumental section, and how to engage the audience with the song and make it interesting and constantly fresh. 

My uniqueness lies in my background in both Indian classical music and Western music, as well as my ability to create a unique blend of these musical styles through words and melody.
DailyTime: Teach music for 5 hours, spend atleast 3 hours on my own music.
MentorName: Learnt Tabla from the legendary Pt. Arvindkumar Azad Guruji. He is the stalwart of the Banaras Gharana of Music and a student of Pt. Kishan Maharaj. Also been mentored by various artists and producers along the way. Learnt the art of mixing & mastering from Josh Clark in the UK, production from Dr. Andy Keep, Sound Design from Prof. Jan Meinema and many more.
CurrentFans: My audience is still developing. Primarily, it is a mix of teenagers, young adults, and people in their 30s. Since till now I've released only emotional heartbreak tracks, my audience ranges from 17-30 year olds who love emotional pop songs.
CurrentSetup:
I've had the pleasure of working with various gears throughout my career. I've worked on some cool pre-amps like the Avalon 737, Neve 1073, SSL SiX, Culture Vulture, SSL Fusion, and many others.

Though my current setup is very modest. I have a Scarlett 2i2, a Steven Slate VSX Headphones for mixing and a Nektar MIDI keyboard for production.

My primary DAW for composition is Ableton Live, and for mixing & mastering, it is ProTools. I have also worked on ambisonics and Dolby Atmos projects using Reaper.
CurrentlyWorkingOn: After the recent release of my 2nd original track, I am now aiming to finish 4 originals by this year. So, I'm primarily working on my singles, collaborating with various artists.

LearningNeeds: I am aiming to build my sonic signature, and I need help understanding how to develop it and find it. I also want to learn more about lyrics writing and the marketing and promotions side of music. I also want to learn how to build my brand as an artist, and how to connect with the right audience and build my community.
MentorshipNeeds: I'm looking for guidance on taking my craft to a grand scale. Right now, I just make music for myself and express my feelings through this art. But I want to build my brand and business and want guidance in those aspects.
CurationNeeds: I definitely need help with my video and artwork. I lack the necessary knowledge to shoot professional-quality music videos. I haven't performed on many live stages, so insights on those would be appreciated too.
FandomNeeds: Better distribution, social media content strategy, & high-level collaborations. I'm not yet able to decode the right content strategy for me. Need help with that.
AspirationalGoal: My 12-month goal is to produce and release atleast 5 professional-grade tracks and gain significant traction through streams and social media. I want to build my brand identity, and my dream collaboration would be with Pritam Da & Shankar-Ehsaan-Loy as they are my idols. Realistically, I love what Garvit-Priyansh are building and would love to be a part of composing and producing their music.
AnythingElse: I want to build a unique space for music, which is genre-less. I feel we are so engrossed with grouping and sorting music into specific genres and labels and titles that we forget its purpose. I want to be able to make music that touches the souls of the people. I want to share my emotions and feelings through music and portray stories that relates to people. So that there can be people who listen to my music and identify themselves in it, and realise that they are not alone in how they feel about life. I aim to build a global community where people understand each other, empathize with each other and feel that they are part of something bigger than themselves.

ArtistIdentity:
​"I am an artist because I believe emotions have no borders. My vision is to use multilingual music as a tool for Cultural Diplomacy. By blending Indian languages with global ones like Korean and English, I want to put India at the center of the global music map, making it a hub similar to how South Korea is for K-pop.
​I believe that when we perform in a host country’s native language as a 'surprise' alongside our Indian roots, it shows deep respect and creates an instant emotional bond. This strategy will not only export Indian music globally but also act as a gateway for the world to explore our culture—boosting tourism and making India a leading global creative hub."

Maine isi vision ko dhyan mein rakhte hue apna pehla tri-lingual song (Hindi-English-Korean) likha hai jo is cultural bridge ka ek live example hai."

I also use AI and modern technology as my creative partners to verify linguistic nuances and compose these diverse emotions, allowing me to create a sound that is both authentic and globally accessible."

This time is spent observing different human emotions, writing lyrics that fit various moods, and using digital tools to learn and refine my songwriting. ArtistIdentity: I am an artist primarily because music provides me  a sense of self fulfilment. the way nothing else does. I  was fortunate enough to born in a family with a rich musical lineage. My grand father, Padmshree Pandit Siyaram Tiwari was a Dhrupad exponent of the Darbhanga gharana. I learnt classical music at an early age and later in life I was drawn to light music genres such as, Ghazal, Sufi and bhajans.
TrainingDetails:
I received formal training in Hindistani classical vocal music since I was 6 years old. I also pursued Sangeet SHiromani from the Department of Music and Fine Arts, University of Delhi. I learnt classical music from various Gurus such as Prof. Sunanda Pathak and Pdt. Bholanath Mishra. I briefly learnt light music from Shrimati Rajashri Pathak (Sandesh Shandilya Sir's Teacher) and Ustad Roshan Bharti as well. 
I give regular broadcasts as a graded artist in Akashvani Delhi, All India Radio in two categories of light music i.e., Bhajan and Ghazal. 

I have composed around 12 ghazals and I have performed them to a large audience as part of a story telling cum live singing concept developed by me called, "Dastan-e-ghazal". It was premiered at Abhivyakti City Arts Project, Ahmedabad in 2026 and was very well received by the audience that comprised of diverse age groups. I have also given performances at different venues of Delhi such as, India Habitat Centre, Depot 48 etc.

ArtistIdentity: I am an artist because music became the only language that could express the thoughts and emotions I could never fully explain in words. From a very young age, I was drawn toward rhythm, storytelling, and emotion. Art was never just a hobby for me it became my way of understanding life, transforming struggles into something meaningful, and connecting with people who feel the same emotions. Creating music gives purpose to my experiences, and every track I make is a reflection of growth, ambition, and truth.
TrainingDetails:
I have formal training in music and hold a Bachelor’s degree in Performing Arts Indian Classical Instrument from the University of Kashmir, where I was also awarded a Gold Medal. While deeply rooted in classical training, I’ve always been drawn toward experimentation and modern sound design.
I began music production in 2019 alongside my journey as a trap and rap artist, blending hip-hop with the expressive nature of the sitar. My style is unconventional and new-wave inspired by classical foundations but not limited by them. I approach the sitar with a contemporary perspective, using it as a voice within modern genres rather than restricting it to tradition alone.
Over the years, I’ve explored multiple genres and developed a sound that reflects versatility and emotion. I don’t believe in limiting myself to one genre; if a sound connects emotionally and creatively, I create it. For me, music is about pushing boundaries while staying authentic.
CoreSkills: My core strength lies in composition, genre-blending, and production arrangement. I naturally create melodies and hooks that connect with the current generation while still carrying emotional depth and individuality. As both a rap artist and producer, I understand how to shape sound creatively — from hard-hitting trap production to experimental textures and layered arrangements. One of my biggest strengths is my instinct for knowing what sound can emotionally connect with listeners while still pushing creative boundaries.
StrengthsUniqueness: My biggest strength is versatility. I don’t limit myself to one identity or one genre. I can move from trap to melodic rap, storytelling, experimental music, pop influences, or high-energy chopper flows while still sounding authentic to myself. My music is driven by emotion, experimentation, and adaptability. Rather than fitting into one niche, I aim to create a world where different sounds, cultures, and emotions can exist together through my artistry
DailyTime: I dedicate most of my day to music in one form or another. Alongside being a full-time artist, I’m currently pursuing my Master’s in Music Production and Sound Engineering because I believe an independent artist should understand every aspect of the creative process. From composing, writing, producing, recording, and sound engineering to learning industry-standard video editing, I’m constantly building my craft. I also practice sitar riyaaz daily for 1–2 hours alongside my studio sessions. Balancing multiple creative roles can slow the journey at times, but I believe in building for the long run. My focus is on growth, consistency, and becoming a complete artist with full creative independence.
MentorName: While I don’t come from a musical lineage, I’ve been deeply guided by my mentor and professor from the University of Kashmir, Yusuf Sir. Under his guidance for the past five years, I trained in Indian classical music, studying ragas, musical theory, and sitar performance in depth. His teachings gave me a strong foundation in discipline, emotion, and musical understanding, which continue to influence the way I create and experiment with modern music today.
CurrentFans:
My current audience mainly consists of listeners between the ages of 17–24, especially people who connect with modern hip-hop, trap, emotional storytelling, and experimental sounds. My strongest listener base is in Srinagar, Mumbai, Delhi, and Bangalore, while I also receive streams from countries like Bangladesh, Pakistan, the United States, and other international regions.
The people who connect with my music usually relate to authenticity, emotion, ambition, and experimentation. My audience appreciates music that blends modern sounds with individuality and cultural influence.
CurrentSetup:
I started my music production journey on FL Studio before eventually transitioning into Logic Pro as my primary DAW. Over the years, I built my own professional recording setup through passion, consistency, and hard work. Today, I operate from a professionally treated studio space that I consider one of the finest personal recording rooms in Kashmir.
My main vocal microphone is the Rode K2, which has become an essential part of my sound. I’ve used multiple microphones and interfaces over time, but I personally prefer the Focusrite Clarett series for recording. Alongside recording vocals and instruments, I also work on production, sound design, mixing, and mastering across different studio setups, allowing me to maintain creative control over my music from start to finish.
CurrentlyWorkingOn:
I recently released a new music video and am currently preparing to drop another one in the coming days. Alongside visuals, I’m consistently creating and scheduling new music to maintain momentum and build a strong catalog. My personal goal for this year is to release at least three tracks every month while continuing to experiment with different sounds and styles.
As an independent artist, one of my biggest challenges is handling visuals completely on my own. I often edit my own videos and manage much of the creative process independently, which has taught me discipline and self-reliance while shaping my artistic vision.
DailyRituals: My daily routine revolves around music and creation. Whether it’s composing, producing, writing lyrics, recording, or sound designing, most of my day is spent building ideas into finished work. I also practice instruments like guitar regularly and continue my sitar riyaaz to stay connected with my musical foundation.
LearningNeeds:
My biggest focus right now is deepening my relationship with the sitar and continuing to develop my own signature playing style. Sitar is an instrument that demands years of dedication, patience, and discipline, and I want to shape it into my strongest artistic weapon.
As a producer and trap artist, I’m constantly experimenting with the fusion of Indian classical textures, Bollywood-inspired emotions, and modern trap soundscapes. Since sound is always evolving, I’m continuously learning and adapting with it.
At the same time, lyricism, composition, and performance are very instinctive and personal for me  they come naturally from lived experiences and emotion rather than formulas.
MentorshipNeeds: I’m mainly looking for mentorship in the business and marketing side of music  understanding how to grow sustainably as an independent artist, build a strong audience, and turn artistry into a long-term career. I already handle much of the creative process myself, so guidance in industry strategy, branding, networking, and positioning would be extremely valuable.
CurationNeeds: I’m confident in my abilities when it comes to music production, recording, and audio curation since I personally produce and shape most of my sound. Where I would truly value support is in the visual side of artistry especially music video production, cinematography, and creative direction for artwork and visuals that can elevate the world around my music.
FandomNeeds:
I believe one of the biggest challenges in today’s trap scene is that audiences often reward noise and controversy faster than authenticity or versatility. My focus has always been the music itself — experimenting with sound, blending genres, and building an identity that is long-lasting rather than momentary.
What I’m looking for is guidance in strategic marketing, audience building, and positioning myself correctly as an artist. I want to understand how to grow organically without compromising my individuality, and how to connect my music with the right audience that truly values experimentation and artistry.
AspirationalGoal:
“In the next 12 months, my goal is to break into a larger national platform  whether through a reality show, major live opportunity, or a breakthrough project that puts my artistry in front of a wider audience. I’ve already auditioned for platforms like Hustle and Legacy because I want to challenge myself at the highest level and understand what truly makes an artist stand out.
At the same time, my biggest commitment is consistency. No matter what happens externally, I’ll continue releasing music, evolving my sound, and building a world around my artistry. For me, success is not just virality  it’s creating a sound and identity that people remember.
AnythingElse:
Honestly, I don’t think an artist’s story can fully fit into forms and sections like these. Behind every release, there are phases of self-doubt, isolation, pressure, and the constant question of whether you’re truly good enough or just chasing something impossible. I’ve experienced moments where I questioned my talent, my direction, and whether this journey would ever lead somewhere meaningful.
But despite all of that, music has always pulled me back. Creating is the only thing that makes me feel fully connected to myself. Every track I make carries a part of my confusion, ambition, pain, confidence, and growth.
I’m not trying to become a copy of what already exists. I’m trying to build an identity that feels honest  even if it takes longer. And maybe that’s what this journey really is continuing to create even when you’re unsure of the outcome.

ArtistIdentity: I've been performing since I was 9 years of age. its all I know. Everything else in life goes on. Music is how, where and when I live.
TrainingDetails: my parents put me into gaana class when i was 9, at Shri Ram Sangeet mahavidyalaya in Raipur, a college affiliated to Khairagarh University. it was a 6 year Vid course that ended when I was in 10th std. meanwhile i also started performing on various stages since i was 9. my parents looked for various opportunities for kids, competitions etc where i thrived. after college (B.E. E&TC), i did a 1 year professional course in Gajendra Singh's saregamapadhanisa academy in Mumbai. did a lot of different things after that. a reality show on Mahua channel, a PG Dip course in direction and scriptwriting from Bharatiya Vidya Bhawan and a brief TV hosting stint on Mahua Plus and DD. when i moved to Mumbai, i assisted Monty Sharma Ji to learn music production. then in covid i started music production on a simple windows setup. Monty Ji introduced me to the entire concept and then i self learnt it. when covid ended, i assisted Meghdeep Bose, a prominent music producer/composer. worked on projects like Brahmastra during that time. then worked with Shameer Tandon Sir as a music supervisor for various ghazal projects. dubbed the most prominent music artists during that time. meanwhile our small effort 'Alfaaz Aur awaaz' by prominent poet Ajay Sahaab Sir, with rajesh Singh and I started gaining a lot of traction. it has been growing since then. Ajay Sir writes oroginal ghazals and new poetry on old classics and I perform them. it is hugely loved worldwide. we did a US tour last year. performed at the royal opera house a month back. it has given me 78k followers on facebook and 32k on Instagram and growing. our youtube channel has 2lakh+ subscribers. hence my taste in great lyrics and poetry also refined with time. and since i've had exposure to stage since childhood, i shine there.
CoreSkills: my pronunciation and way to emote is my USP. that is why people like me. i get infinite comments saying my voice gives them 'sukoon', is soulful, connect with emotions very strongly.
StrengthsUniqueness: correct pronunciation and emoting as priority. i don't 'try' to sound contemporary, i don't mince words in my mouth, i don't overdo nakkashi. my singing is simple and straight from the heart into the heart.
DailyTime: no, its not fixed. some days it'll be 4-5 hours of riyaaz and listening and learning and conceptualisation. someday absolutely nothing. not a sound. i rarely listen to new music. but also, something or the other is always in the head.
MentorName: not yet. not in person i mean. but LataJi, Asha Ji, Kishore Kumar, Hemant Kumar, Madam Mohan sahab, SD Burman, RDB.. each of those legends have been GURUS.
CurrentFans: lovers of old songs, meaningful lyrics and ghazals. mostly parents' generation, but instagram/facebook insights show audience age from 18-35 as  highest.
CurrentSetup: logic pro, a simple home studio with sound card, mic, monitors etc.
CurrentlyWorkingOn: haven't made any new music in the last couple of years, had a baby :)
DailyRituals: work out, trying to get back into shape postpartum.
LearningNeeds: making better, more engaging content
MentorshipNeeds: how to scale myself to the next level. more recognisability, talent/artist management, record deals, shows
CurationNeeds: yes. live stage design and entire identity
FandomNeeds: high level collaborations, tours, contracts with talent/artist management, content strategy and most importantly, audios on streaming platforms
AspirationalGoal: in the next 12 months, i want to establish myself as the most heard/followed/watched new talent with houseful shows across prestigious venues in the world. i want to be the new soulful voice of this generation.
AnythingElse: i want to do solid lyrics based music that has depth. but also, across genres. even dance numbers can have great lyrics, great compositions and great tracks. i just don't want to do music that goes with the trend, sound like a particular genre or is a remake with a new sound just for the sake of it. it needs to last. it needs to touch people on the inside, evoke emotions. its happening already. i want to take it to the next level.
